notes: with_skill achieved 100% pass rate across all 3 evals (16/16 assertions passed)
without_skill scored 88% (14/16) — failed naming_convention for token_tracker and completeness for extract.py
Key skill advantages: consistent directory structure (examples/<module>/<func>.py), correct ClassName_method.py naming for class methods, strict 1:1 function-to-file mapping
The 'runnable' and 'meaningful_inputs' assertions pass 100% in both configurations — non-discriminating
with_skill is actually faster on average (339s vs 392s) despite using slightly more tokens (57K vs 53K), suggesting more focused execution
Bonus finding: both configurations independently discovered a deadlock bug in TokenTracker.save_report (Lock vs RLock issue)
